How is NEXBRIDGE regulated?

Independent, regulated custodians hold segregated, issuance-specific reserves. Controls include dual-control on NAV and mint/burn operations, documented time-stamped approvals, daily reconciliations, and monthly independent assurance attestations.

Like traditional ETFs, NEXBRIDGE tokens track reference markets under clear, published rules. They offer transparency through documented rescaling mechanisms, disclosed fees, fair NAV-based subscriptions/redemptions, regulatory clarity at the token level, proof of reserves, institutional settlement rails, and liquidity focus through OTC and selective exchange listings.

Liquid Network provides Bitcoin-anchored assurance, policy-based controls for regulated issuance, predictable efficient settlement, mature security models, and confidential-transaction capabilities reducing front-running risks while preserving verifiability.

NEXBRIDGE is a regulated issuer of digital assets structured with an ETF-like design, based on published rules, and issued natively on Bitcoin's Liquid Network. Each token includes a public term sheet defining fees, rules, risks, and investor rights with dedicated registration numbers.

OTC fees appear in OTC Desk T&Cs and each issuance's RID; exchange fees vary by venue with spreads depending on order-book liquidity. No hidden costs; all rules and fees are published in the RID and venue fee schedules.

Phase 1 (current): Pre-fund orders, execute at end-of-day NAV with T+1 token activation. Redemptions lock tokens at T and process payouts T+1 at NAV. Phase 2 (upcoming): RFQ for larger requests and optional collateralized margin wallets supporting faster settlement.

Primary market access targets professional/institutional investors (funds, banks, brokers, family offices) requiring KYC/AML and address whitelisting. Secondary market access for self-directed users in eligible jurisdictions depends on exchange-specific rules and jurisdiction restrictions.
Questions

Running the OTC Desk with expanding features (best execution, RFQ, margin funding), expanding authorized self-custody wallets, rolling out selective CEX listings with professional market makers, and publishing updates on regulated stablecoin integration.
Still have questions?

The canonical issuance exists on Bitcoin technology as the single source of truth. Future access wrappers on other chains would mirror the same RID rules, maintain 1:1 supply ratios, and settle back to the canonical chain.

Tokens remain in either self-custody through approved whitelisted addresses (like Blockstream Green/Jade) or exchange custody at regulated CEXs listing the issuance. Transfers only occur between approved addresses.

USTBL functions as an on-chain fixed-income benchmark, gateway for institutional de-risk strategies, liquidity anchor for market-making/lending, structured notes foundation, and pays clear integrated yield to holders.

USTBL provides short-term fixed-income digital asset exposure (Treasury-style) issued on Bitcoin infrastructure, delivering transparent integrated yield and serving as a benchmark, liquidity anchor, and structured product foundation.

100% of USTBL tokens are supported by short-term U.S. Treasury ETFs (ISIN: IE00BGSF1X88), providing investors with direct exposure to U.S. Treasury bond performance. This structure ensures that the token's value is anchored to one of the most trusted and liquid asset classes in global markets.

NexBridge Digital Financial Solutions has partnered with Grant Thornton to conduct monthly independent audits of the reserves backing the USTBL token. This collaboration operates under El Salvador's regulatory framework and is designed to ensure the highest standards of transparency and investor protection.
NexBridge and Grant Thornton Announce Ongoing Collaboration for Monthly USTBL Reserve Certification

The partnership ensures that all outstanding USTBL tokens remain fully backed by liquid and verifiable assets at all times. Each month, Grant Thornton independently verifies the assets held in segregated accounts and validates the token's Net Asset Value (NAV), providing an additional layer of assurance for token holders.

As part of its commitment to the Bitcoin ecosystem, NexBridge joined the Liquid Federation, a consortium of over 70 Bitcoin-focused companies and exchanges that collectively maintain and govern the Liquid Network.
NexBridge Launches USTBL: The First Regulated Tokenized U.S. Treasury on Bitcoin Technology

NexBridge announced the launch of USTBL — the first regulated public offering of tokenized U.S. Treasury exposure built on Bitcoin technology. USTBL aims to democratize access to U.S. Treasury returns by leveraging the security and efficiency of blockchain infrastructure.

The launch of USTBL comes at a time of accelerating institutional interest in tokenized real-world assets. Research from BCG projects that the tokenized real-world assets market could reach $16 trillion by 2030, reflecting the significant potential for blockchain-based financial products.

USTBL is issued under El Salvador's Digital Asset Law, with NexBridge registered with the National Commission of Digital Assets (CNAD) as a licensed digital asset issuer. The token launched on Bitfinex Securities, the first platform licensed as a Digital Asset Service Provider (DASP) under Salvadoran regulations.

USTBL operates on Bitcoin's Liquid Network, utilizing Blockstream's Asset Management Platform (AMP). The AMP framework enforces transfer restrictions at the protocol level, ensuring that only KYC/AML-verified investors can hold and transfer tokens. This design embeds compliance directly into the token infrastructure.

USTBL represents the initial step in NexBridge's broader product roadmap. The company plans to introduce equity-based products in the near term, before expanding to develop new financial instruments that are not currently available through traditional market infrastructure.
ustbl-reaches-goal: "This milestone represents more than a financial achievement — it reflects the strength of NexBridge's vision and execution."
"We are bridging the gap between blockchain technology and government-backed digital assets."
NexBridge Reaches Initial $30 Million Soft Cap in Record Time for Tokenized U.S. Treasury Bills
NexBridge reached its $30 million soft cap for USTBL within just 48 hours of launch, demonstrating strong market demand for regulated, blockchain-based Treasury exposure.

The Liquid Network currently holds over $2 billion in real-world assets (RWAs), establishing it as a significant platform for tokenized financial products. USTBL's rapid uptake further validates the network's role in institutional-grade digital asset issuance.

USTBL gives global investors blockchain-based access to U.S. Treasury returns while maintaining full regulatory compliance. The record-setting soft cap achievement underscores the growing demand for transparent, regulated digital asset products built on proven infrastructure.

USTBL operates on Bitcoin's Liquid Network, combining the trusted stability of U.S. Treasuries with the efficiency of blockchain settlement. Following the initial subscription period, the offering remained open through November 29, with bi-weekly subscription windows established thereafter to accommodate ongoing investor interest.
— Delivers foundational platform support as the first licensed Digital Asset Service Provider under El Salvador's regulations, enabling investor access to USTBL.
— Provides the AMP (Asset Management Platform) and Liquid Network infrastructure that powers USTBL's issuance, transfer restrictions, and compliance enforcement.
ustbl-rid-update-2025: Audited financial statements have been added to the documentation, reinforcing NexBridge's commitment to transparency. The Terms and Conditions have also been updated to provide clarified token holder rights, ensuring all participants have clear and comprehensive information about their positions.

Implementing this change requires a token migration process: existing tokens will be burned and replaced with new tokens supporting the expanded decimal precision. Bitfinex Securities, which currently holds 100% of issued tokens, will coordinate the migration process. The migration is scheduled for the end of September 2025.

It is important to note that this migration will not affect the nominal value, pricing, or token holder rights associated with USTBL. The change is purely technical in nature and enhances the token's functionality without altering its economic characteristics.

NexBridge has published an updated Relevant Information Document (RID) for USTBL, following its quarterly and semi-annual update cycle as reviewed by the National Commission of Digital Assets (CNAD). This update reflects several important changes to the token's structure, documentation, and technical capabilities.

The most significant update in this release is the increase in decimal support from 0 to 6 decimal places. This technical enhancement enables fractional transfers of USTBL tokens, substantially improving operational efficiency and preparing the ground for new use cases within the ecosystem.

The updated RID includes a revised yield to maturity for the underlying investment, reflecting current market conditions. Additionally, the issuer description has been enhanced to accurately reflect NexBridge's CNAD registration as both a licensed digital asset issuer and a Digital Assets Service Provider (DASP).
The updated RID is publicly available at  and , where an archive of previous versions is also maintained for reference.
ustbl: NexBridge Launches First Regulated Public Offering of Tokenized U.S. Treasury Bills

NexBridge has joined the Liquid Federation, a consortium of over 70 members including Bitcoin companies and exchanges. The project represents a close collaboration between NexBridge, Blockstream (led by Dr. Adam Back), and Bitfinex Securities — combining expertise in digital asset issuance, Bitcoin infrastructure, and regulated exchange operations.

NexBridge launched USTBL — the first regulated public offering of tokenized U.S. Treasury exposure on Bitcoin technology, with an initial soft cap of $30 million. The product is designed to bring the stability and yield of U.S. Treasuries to a global investor base through blockchain infrastructure.
The launch of USTBL is expected to increase total real-world assets (RWAs) on the Liquid Network to over $2 billion, representing more than 15% of the blockchain-based RWA market.

USTBL aims to address the accessibility and cost barriers that have traditionally limited participation in Treasury markets. By reducing intermediaries and associated fees through blockchain-based settlement, the product works to democratize access to stable, regulated financial assets for a broader range of global investors.

USTBL is built on Bitcoin's Liquid Network using Blockstream's Asset Management Platform (AMP). This technology stack enables standardized compliance rules that can be enforced across jurisdictions, embedding KYC/AML verification directly at the token level to ensure only approved investors can participate.

USTBL is issued under El Salvador's Digital Asset Law, providing a clear and established regulatory framework for the offering. Bitfinex Securities, which became El Salvador's first Digital Asset License holder in 2023, serves as the listing platform for USTBL, connecting the token to a regulated exchange infrastructure.
